subject: [PATCH v3 05/16] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch adds two new flags to the change_protection() function in the Linux kernel: MM_CP_UFFD_RWP and MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE. These flags are used for userfaultfd (UFFD) read-write protection, which is a mechanism that allows userspace to protect memory ranges from being accessed by other processes. The MM_CP_UFFD_RWP flag installs PAGE_NONE with the uffd PTE bit set, while the MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE flag restores the original vma->vm_page_prot and clears the uffd bit. This patch also updates several kernel functions to support these new flags, including change_pte_range(), change_huge_pmd(), and hugetlb_change_protection(). The goal of this patch is to provide a consistent and efficient way for UFFD to protect memory ranges in the kernel.
